rulings.Case Title: JAMELLAH SULUKI VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Joint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS JOHN STEWART COMPANY, INC. AND 1340 HUDSON AVENUE, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants 1340 Hudson Avenue, L.P. and John Stewart Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. The threshold issue is how to frame the alleged duty, if any, the Property Defendants owed to Assad Gibson vis-à-vis defendant DeAngela Grayson's vehicle. Because the Property Defendants are the owner and operator of the apartment complex, they owed Assad and all other tenants a duty "to use reasonable care to keep the property in a reasonably safe condition," which entails the "use of reasonable care to discover any unsafe conditions and to repair, replace, or give adequate warning of anything that could be reasonably expected to harm others." (CACI 1001). This basic duty is enhanced and expanded as applied to the facts of this case for at least two reasons. First, "a greater degree of care is generally owed to children because of their lack of capacity to appreciate risks and avoid danger." (McDaniel v. Sunset Manor Co. (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d. 1, 7.) Second, San Francisco Transportation Code 5.1 explicitly provides that "wrecked" or "inoperative vehicles ¿ constitute an attractive nuisance creating a hazard to the health and safety of minors ¿ [T]he presence of ¿ [a] wrecked ¿ or inoperative vehicle ¿ on private property ¿ is hereby declared to constitute a public nuisance." Per a very recent case decided by the First District, the proper way to frame what duty, if any, the Property Defendants owed to Assad with respect to Ms. Grayson's vehicle is to ask the question whether the Property Defendants had a duty to protect Assad from the risk posed by Ms. Grayson's vehicle. (Staats v. Vintner's Golf Club, LLC (Cal. App. August 1, 2018) 2018 WL 3640830). Because it is reasonably foreseeable that the presence of a wrecked and inoperable vehicle on the premises of the apartment complex where a minor child lives could cause injury to the child and the imposition of a duty on the owner and operator of the complex to protect a child from that danger is supported by the "policy factors," as a matter of law the Property Defendants "owed a duty to protect ¿ [Assad] from the risk posed by" the presence of Ms. Grayson's vehicle on the premises they owned and operated. (Id). Once that duty is established, Staats further holds that "which actions the ¿ [Property Defendants] should have taken to minimize the risk (including the extent of reasonable inspections), whether the ¿ [Property Defendants] did take those actions, and whether any failure to do so proximately caused ¿ [Assad's death] are all questions for the trier of fact" and not amenable to resolution on a motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Because the Property Defendants' motion did not frame the duty issue in the correct way and narrowly limited it only to consideration whether they had a duty to tow the vehicle, the Property Defendants did not satisfy their summary motion burden of presenting sufficient facts to establish that they owed no duty to Assad. But even if they had properly characterized the duty they owed to Assad, Staats makes clear that the Property Defendants are unable to prevail on a no duty argument on a summary motion. (Continued in Part 2 of 2)=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMELLAH SULUKI VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of 2 Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS JOHN STEWART COMPANY, INC. AND 1340 HUDSON AVENUE, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Part 2 Of 2 Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) Assuming without deciding that the Property Defendants presented sufficient facts to establish that they did not have the legal authority to tow Ms. Grayson's vehicle once the vehicle had been towed to their complex, the Property Defendants did not present any facts showing why they could not have refused to allow the vehicle to be towed to the complex and/or why the vehicle could not have been placed in the carport with its front end first and/or why the Property defendants could not have taken any other actions to minimize the risk of danger to Assad such as making it harder to access or move the vehicle and/or why the Property Defendants did not make a reasonable inspection of the vehicle (including the TSCI) to determine whether it could be accessed and moved by a child. As stated in Staats, all these issues are factual issues which are ordinarily not amenable to resolution on a summary motion. None of those issues are amenable to resolution on this motion for the simple and basic reason that the Property Defendants provided no facts about any of them. Nor have the Property Defendants provided sufficient admissible facts to satisfy their summary burden as to causation and, even if they had, there is a triable dispute whether the asserted breach of their duty to protect Assad from the risk posed by Ms. Grayson's vehicle was a substantial factor in causing Assad's death and/or the assertedly malfunctioning TSCI was a superseding cause. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Suluki is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: EVAN KROW VS. SAUL GEVERTZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Saul Gevertz's demurrer to all five causes of action alleged in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is sustained without leave to amend as to all five causes of action. The third amended complaint fails to allege a valid claim against Mr. Gevertz for either direct or alter ego liability. The allegations in the third amended complaint are not substantively different from the allegations in the second amended complaint. The addition of the conspiracy allegations does not create liability. "When a corporate employee acts in the course of his or her employment, on behalf of the corporation, there is no entity apart from the employee with whom the employee can conspire. [I]t is basic in the law of conspiracy that you must have two persons or entities to have a conspiracy. A corporation cannot conspire with itself any more than a private individual can, and it is the general rule that the acts of the agent are the acts of the corporation...." (Black v. Bank of America (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 1, 6 (internal quotations omitted).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gevertz is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES GAYAPA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Court Orders And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce The Court Orders And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for an order to enforce sanctions is unopposed and granted to the extent plaintiff shall have twenty days from the date of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry by both mail and email to serve code compliant verified responses, without objection, to all discovery requests which are the subject of the motion. This is the final opportunity for plaintiff to comply with the two previous Orders of this Court. If the plaintiff fails to comply he shall have no further opportunities to respond and the Court will entertain a motion to dismiss his action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SKANSKA USA CIVIL WEST CALIFORNIA DISTRICT, INC., VS. WEBCOR BUILDERS/OBAYASHI CORP., A JOINT VENTURE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's demurrer to the sixth cause of action in the amended complaint for violation of San Francisco Administrative Code 6.22(j) is sustained without leave to amend. Government Code 905 requires that all claims for money or damages against a public entity be presented to that public entity prior to filing a lawsuit. Pleading compliance with the section 905 or an excuse for noncompliance, which applies to both contract and tort claims, is an element of plaintiff Skanska USA Civil West California District, Inc.'s section 6.22(j) claim. (State v. Superior Crt. (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1240 ("We conclude that failure to allege facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the claim presentation requirement subjects a claim against a public entity to a demurrer for failure to state a cause of action"); City of Stockton v. Superior Crt. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 734 ("we hold that these requirements apply to breach of contract claims.")). Skanska seeks "money damages" from TJPA, which is a public entity. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend because there is no indication in the papers on this demurrer that Skanska may be able to allege either compliance with section 905 or an excuse for noncompliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for TJPA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: BENITO NUNEZ VS. 1500 MAJESTIC, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTS 1500 MAJESTIC, LLC AND VIKAS PATEL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants 1500 Majestic LLC and Vikas Patel's demurrer to the eleventh cause of action for violation of Bus. & Prof. Code17200 and the request for punitive damages in the complaint filed by plaintiff Benito Nunez's is overruled in its entirety. Mr. Nunez has alleged sufficient facts supporting standing to sue in his individual capacity for violation of section 17200 and he may seek a restitutionary remedy for recovery of unpaid wages per Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 177. A pleading challenge to a request for punitive damages should be made by a motion to strike, not a demurrer. In all events, Mr. Nunez has alleged sufficient facts showing malicious and fraudulent conduct by defendants supporting a punitive damages award based on his tort claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for Mr. Nunez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CHUKWUDIFU EBOCHIE VS. PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANTS PANCHITA'S RESTAURANT LLC AND DORIS CAMPOS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Panchita's Restaurant LLC and Doris Campos' motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by Chukwudifu Ebochie is granted without leave to amend. Mr. Ebochie does not allege any facts showing employer liability for punitive damages against Panchita's and it does not appear that at this stage of the case Mr. Ebohcie is able to do so. The allegation that Panchita's may have ratified the misconduct of its employee is insufficient to support employer liability for punitive damages. Nor does Mr. Ebochie allege that Ms. Campos acted with malice, fraud or oppression and it does not appear that at this stage of the case Mr. Ebohcie is able to do so. In the event that Mr. Ebochie learns facts that support punitive damages liability against Panchita's and/or Ms. Campos, Mr. Ebochie may file a motion seeking leave to amend to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: GRASS ROOTS NONPROFIT COLLECTIVE, INC. VS. JULIE YOON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Superior Court Case With Pending Small Claims Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 21, 2018, Line 15, PLAINTIFF GRASS ROOTS NONPROFIT COLLECTIVE, INC.'S Motion To Transfer And Consolidate Superior Court Case With Pending Small Claims Case. Plaintiff Grass Roots Nonprofit Collective, Inc.'s motion to consolidate this case with the small claims case number 858229 is granted. Case 858229 is transferred out of the Small Claims Division. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. This case is the lead case and all further filings will be in this case only. The August 28, 2018 trial date set in case 858229 is vacated. Defendant Julie Yoon is permitted to file a cross-complaint in this case no later than August 31, 2018 re-pleading her claim that she alleged in case 858229 and in doing so she is not limited to the jurisdictional maximum amount of Small Claims Court. There is no persuasive reason why Ms. Yoon should be required to file a separate motion to obtain such permission when there is no persuasive reason why she should not be given such permission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Yoon is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
